channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: South Africans face steep fuel price hikes from Wednesday
item.description: The Department of Mineral and Petroleum Resources has confirmed major fuel price increases set to take effect from Wednesday. Motorists across South Africa will see the price of petrol rise by R3.06 p...

item.title: How Homeowners Can Maximise the Financial Value of Their Rooftops
item.description: For many Australians, rooftops have long been seen simply as protection from the sun and rain or a place for solar panels. However, recent data from the Australian Rooftop Valuation Report reveals tha...

item.title: Tension rises over redevelopment plans at Coco Beach Dar es Salaam
item.description: Coco Beach, one of Dar es Salaam’s most famous public beaches, has once again become a battleground of emotions and disagreements following new efforts to redevelop it. The renewed debate erupted when...

item.title: Farm Worker Killed by Co-Worker in General Santos City
item.description: Authorities in General Santos City are actively searching for a suspect in the killing of farm worker Bilmar Marco Danieles Sr. The victim was discovered dead on March 31, 2026, inside a deteriorated ...

item.title: Key Headlines on Middle East Conflict, Philippine Fuel Supply, and Space Exploration
item.description: Today’s news highlights major international and local developments. In Dubai, a Kuwaiti crude oil tanker was set ablaze following an attack by Iran, which also caused damage to its hull. Meanwhile, in...

item.title: Jozi FM Board Chair's Lottery Fraud Case Escalated to Specialised Commercial Crimes Unit
item.description: Rapitse Montsho, chairperson of Jozi FM's board, faces allegations of fraud and theft involving almost R35-million in National Lottery Commission grants. Although charges were provisionally withdrawn ...

item.title: Johannesburg pothole and water leak finally repaired after long-standing complaints
item.description: Residents of Douglasdale in northern Johannesburg have finally seen repairs made to a large pothole and persistent water leak outside their homes after more than three years of repeated complaints. Th...

item.title: Tanzania strengthens national measures to fight antimicrobial resistance
item.description: The Tanzanian government is taking stronger steps to tackle antimicrobial resistance (AMR), locally called ‘UVIDA’. During the 32nd meeting of the National Coordinating Committee on AMR held in Dar es...

item.title: Vodacom Tanzania invests $28 Million to upgrade M-Pesa platform for faster and safer digital payments
item.description: Vodacom Tanzania is making a big move in the country’s digital economy by investing $28 million to modernise its M-Pesa platform. With mobile money now a central part of life for Tanzanians, the upgra...

item.title: Micro Observers Allocated to Sensitive Polling Booths in Thoothukudi District
item.description: The Election Commission of India has successfully completed the initial randomisation process for allocating micro observers to 246 sensitive polling booths across 203 polling stations in six Assembly...

item.title: Global Renewable Energy Capacity Approaches Half of Total Electricity Production in 2025
item.description: In 2025, renewable energy sources accounted for nearly 50 per cent of the world's electricity capacity, driven primarily by a record surge in solar installations. According to data from the Internatio...

item.title: SM City Zamboanga: A Mall Celebrating Local Heritage Through Architecture
item.description: SM Supermalls has officially opened SM City Zamboanga, its 90th mall in the Philippines, which showcases the rich cultural heritage of the Zamboanga Peninsula through modern architecture. Located alon...

item.title: Deputy President asks for restoration of SHA portal tracking payments to health facilities
item.description: Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua has urged the National Assembly Majority Leader, Aden Duale, to restore the SHA (Single Health Account) portal that previously provided transparency on how monthly pa...

item.title: UK housing market faces pressure from rising mortgage and energy costs
item.description: The UK housing market is anticipated to soften as households grapple with rising mortgage and energy costs, driven by the ongoing conflict in Iran, Nationwide has indicated. Despite March showing a 0....

item.title: Pakistan and China propose five-point peace plan amid Iran conflict
item.description: Pakistan and China jointly called for an immediate ceasefire and early launch of peace negotiations to end the ongoing US-Israeli war on Iran, stressing the need to prevent further escalation and safe...

item.title: 'Transcription' by Ben Lerner: Technology, Memory and the Intricacies of Human Connection
item.description: Ben Lerner's novel 'Transcription' weaves an intricate tapestry exploring the intersections of technology, memory, and human connection. The narrative follows a middle-aged American narrator who trave...

item.title: Trump urges other nations to secure oil in the Strait of Hormuz without US help
item.description: US President Donald Trump has suggested that nations not involved in the ongoing Middle East conflict should 'go get your own oil' in the Strait of Hormuz, as reported on his Truth Social account. He ...

item.title: Air Canada challenges $426K fine over passenger refund and rebooking violations during 2025 strike
item.description: Air Canada has announced it will contest a $426,000 fine imposed by the Canadian Transportation Agency (CTA) following 71 alleged violations of the Air Passenger Protection Regulations (APPR). These v...

item.title: Astronomers Observe Unprecedented Long-Lasting Gamma-Ray Burst with Webb Telescope
item.description: Astronomers have observed an extraordinary cosmic event, GRB 250702B, that challenges current understanding of stellar explosions. Detected by NASA's James Webb Space Telescope along with a network of...

item.title: TANTRADE officials visit Kwala Dry Port to observe operations and cargo handling
item.description: A group of 50 staff from Tanzania Trade Development Authority (TANTRADE), led by Deputy Director General Freddy Liundi, recently visited Kwala Dry Port in Vigwaza, Kibaha, Coast Region. The aim of the...
